pattern: Track the element with the smallest absolute value, breaking ties toward the larger signed value so a positive beats its negative twin.
hints: Compare elements by their distance from zero, which is their absolute value.
When two candidates are equally close, the tie rule prefers the bigger signed number, so a positive wins over its negative counterpart.
Scan once, keeping the best absolute value seen and, on a tie, the larger of the two original values.

edge_inputs.description: tie between 1 and -1 picks the positive
negative is strictly closer than any positive
all at the magnitude extreme, both signs tie

stress.description: random arrays that frequently produce sign ties at the closest magnitude
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-50, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
